top_signals.title: European Gas Prices Rise
top_signals.summary: European natural gas futures rose to around €47.1 per megawatt hour, recouping part of the previous session’s losses amid continued lack of any concrete resolution to the Middle East conflict despite recent optimism. Iranian media reported progress on a draft deal aimed at ending the war, though the White House later dismissed the claim as ...
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: European defense stocks rally after Ukraine ratifies $105 billion EU loan deal; regional indices fall
top_signals.summary: European stocks were broadly lower on Thursday as investors assessed news related to the Iran war and developments in Ukraine.

top_signals.title: Landmark report reveals labour social rights gap for sea workers in Europe
top_signals.summary: On 27 May, a major new report titled “Charting the Distinct Rights of Sea Workers in European Waters: A Focus on Decent Working Time”, was released by the World Maritime University (WMU) commissioned by the European Transport Workers’ Federation (ETF) and the International Transport Workers’ Federation (ITF). The study is primarily driven by the differential ...

top_signals.title: First Feasibility Study on Maritime Nuclear Generation in Greece Identifies No Fundamental Barriers to Implementation
top_signals.summary: Deon Policy Institute has published the first ever report from research examining how Floating Nuclear Power Plants (FNPPs) could be considered within the political, social, economic, policy and regulatory context of a European country, using Greece as a case study, available here. The policy insights are derived from a research programme conducted by CORE POWER, ...

top_signals.title: Chinese steel flows face ongoing and new headwinds
top_signals.summary: Steel flows slip into negative growth in April 2026 • Global steel flows soften 8% y/y in April 2026. • Flows originating in China increased proportionality to 45%, from a log-run average of 38%, but in tonnage terms, fell by 4% to 10mt. • Flows from outside of China have decreased by 11%. • Indonesia ...

top_signals.title: Pokemon cards’ most enthusiastic collectors? Money launderers
top_signals.summary: The market for Pokemon cards it absolutely booming, but there's a more sinister force than nerdy millennials at play, writes Paul Ormerod.

top_signals.title: EU Fertiliser Action Plan signals long-term reform; offers little immediate relief after Hormuz disruption
top_signals.summary: The European Commission on 19 May announced its Fertiliser Action Plan, combining largely pre-announced measures to support farmer affordability with longer-term signals on market transparency, strategic autonomy, and the decarbonisation of EU fertilizer production. The plan was published with EU nitrogen fertilizer prices that the Commission estimated to be around 70% above the 2024 average ...

top_signals.title: Supply contracts and spot bunker sales both on table for Oman project – HutanBio
top_signals.summary: UK-based biofuel startup HutanBio plans to expand algae-based biofuel production in Oman to 8,000 mt/year by 2029, with offtaker Wakud open to both long-term shipowner contracts and spot bunker sales, chief executive Manshu Agarwal told ENGINE. Last week, HutanBio announced a partnership with Oman’s Utopia World Investment (UWI) to commercialise and expand biofuel production in ...
